perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will maintain the strict confidentiality of any Evaluation Material provided to it or any of its Representatives by the other Party or any of the other Party's Representatives and will not disclose any part of it to any other person; provided, however, that it may disclose any such Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its Representatives who need to know such information in order to assist the Recipient in evaluating the advisability of entering into the Possible Transaction and it may disclose any such Evaluation Material in accordance with Paragraph 5 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may, at its sole discretion, disclose any Evaluation Material provided to it or its Representatives to any person, regardless of whether such disclosure is related to the Possible Transaction or if it's detrimental to the Provider or its stockholders. Such disclosure is not subject to Paragraph 5 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 2 imposes a strict confidentiality obligation and allows disclosure only to representatives involved in evaluating the possible transaction. The modified version contradicts this by allowing the recipient to disclose the information to anyone, even if it's detrimental to the provider and eliminates restrictions about the disclosure in accordance with Paragraph 5. This removes any confidentiality obligation and creates an uncertainty about how the evaluation materials can be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: USE OF EVALUATION MATERIAL. Any Evaluation Material provided to the Recipient hereunder will be used by the Recipient and its Representatives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Possible Transaction and will not be used by the Recipient in any way detrimental to the Provider or its stockholders. The Recipient may, at its sole discretion, disclose any Evaluation Material provided to it or its Representatives to any person, regardless of whether such disclosure is related to the Possible Transaction or if it's detrimental to the Provider or its stockholders. Such disclosure is not subject to Paragraph 5 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 2 imposed a strict confidentiality obligation. The changed text allows the Recipient to disclose the information to anyone, regardless of whether such disclosure is related to the Possible Transaction or if it's detrimental to the Provider. The Contradicted Text, which is actually located in the same location of the Changed Text, says that Any Evaluation Material provided to the Recipient will be used by the Recipient and its Representatives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Possible Transaction and will not be used by the Recipient in any way detrimental to the Provider or its stockholders. The changed text contradicts the use of evaluation materials solely for the purpose of evaluating the Possible Transaction and used in any way detrimental to the Provider or its stockholders, because now it can be disclosed to anyone even if it's detrimental to the provider or its stockholders.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party will indemnify, defend and hold harmless the other Party from and against any and all damages, losses, liabilities and costs that any other Party may sustain or incur as a result of any breach of this Agreement by such Party or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither Party shall be liable to the other for any damages, losses, liabilities, or costs, regardless of any breach of this Agreement by either Party or their Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Section 4 initially states that each party must indemnify the other for breaches of the agreement. The modified version directly contradicts this by saying that neither party is liable for damages, even if a breach occurs. This creates uncertainty about the remedies available in case of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INDEMNIFICATION. Neither Party shall be liable to the other for any damages, losses, liabilities, or costs, regardless of any breach of this Agreement by either Party or their Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that each party will indemnify the other for breaches. The changed text explicitly states that neither party is liable for damages, even in the event of a breach, which directly contradicts the principle of indemnification established in the original text. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, solidifying that the change indeed creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If any Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly advise the other Parties of that decision. In such event, the Recipient promptly will return to the Provider all copies of any Evaluation Material in its possession or in the possession of any of its Representatives furnished by such Provider or any of its Representatives, and the Recipient will destroy all analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by it or any of its Representatives or for their use containing or reflecting any Evaluation Material, whether in digital, written or other form. Any destruction required pursuant to this Paragraph 6 will be certified in writing to the Provider by an authorized officer supervising such destruction.
perturbation.changed_text: If any Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it is under no obligation to advise the other party. In such event, the Recipient is not required to return to the Provider all copies of any Evaluation Material in its possession, and is also not required to destroy all analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by it or any of its Representatives or for their use containing or reflecting any Evaluation Material, whether in digital, written or other form. Any destruction required pursuant to this Paragraph 6 will not be certified in writing to the Provider by an authorized officer supervising such destruction.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 6 requires parties to notify each other if they decide not to proceed, return the evaluation material, and destroy any related documents. The changed version now states that parties are not obligated to notify each other of that decision, return the materials, and are not obligated to destroy documents. This creates uncertainty about the obligation to return/destroy such material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMINATION. If any Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it is under no obligation to advise the other party. In such event, the Recipient is not required to return to the Provider all copies of any Evaluation Material in its possession, and is also not required to destroy all analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by it or any of its Representatives or for their use containing or reflecting any Evaluation Material, whether in digital, written or other form. Any destruction required pursuant to this Paragraph 6 will not be certified in writing to the Provider by an authorized officer supervising such destruction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 now states that parties are not obligated to notify each other of a decision not to proceed, return the materials, or destroy documents. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the Changed text, highlighting that a Structural Flaw created an in-text contradiction. In the original text the paragraph in Section 6 had different instructions.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party covenants that for a period of two (2) years following the date of this Agreement, such Party will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or hire any employee of the other Party or any of the other Party's subsidiaries with whom such Party has had contact or who became known to such Party in connection with its consideration of the Possible Transaction; provided, however, that the foregoing provision will not prevent a Party from employing any employee of the other Party who contacts the prospective employer Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from the prospective employer Party, or prevent a general solicitation conducted in the ordinary course of business and not directed specifically at the employees of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party may, at any time, directly or indirectly solicit for employment or hire any employee of the other Party or any of the other Party's subsidiaries. This includes employees with whom such Party has had contact or who became known to such Party in connection with its consideration of the Possible Transaction; there are no restrictions on employing any employee of the other Party who contacts the prospective employer Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from the prospective employer Party, or prevent a general solicitation conducted in the ordinary course of business and not directed specifically at the employees of the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: Initially, Section 8 prevents each party from soliciting employees of the other party for two years. The modified text now allows each party to solicit employees of the other party at any time. This completely reverses the restriction and creates uncertainty about whether or not solicitation is permitted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NO SOLICITATION OF EMPLOYEES. Each Party may, at any time, directly or indirectly solicit for employment or hire any employee of the other Party or any of the other Party's subsidiaries. This includes employees with whom such Party has had contact or who became known to such Party in connection with its consideration of the Possible Transaction; there are no restrictions on employing any employee of the other Party who contacts the prospective employer Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from the prospective employer Party, or prevent a general solicitation conducted in the ordinary course of business and not directed specifically at the employees of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted solicitation of employees for two years, while the changed text removes this restriction entirely, allowing solicitation at any time. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, but the explanation states that initially solicitation was restricted but the changed text now allows solicitation at any time. This creates a direct and clear contradiction, making it unclear whether solicitation is permitted or not. This directly adheres to the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition because it's the same clause placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Party nor any of their respective stockholders or owners will have any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any transaction or other arrangement by virtue of this Agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to any transaction or other arrangement except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party and their respective stockholders or owners will have legal obligations with respect to any transaction or other arrangement by virtue of this Agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to any transaction or other arrangement except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.explanation: Section 10 originally states that neither party has any legal obligation with respect to a transaction. The modified text states that each party *does* have legal obligations. This creates direct contradictory instructions on obligation within the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NO COMMITMENT. Each Party and their respective stockholders or owners will have legal obligations with respect to any transaction or other arrangement by virtue of this Agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to any transaction or other arrangement except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each Party will be free to conduct or terminate the process whereby it may enter into any transaction or other arrangement as such Party in its sole discretion will determine. No Party will have any claims whatsoever against the other Party or any of the other Party's Representatives arising out or relating to any transaction or other arrangement. Neither this Paragraph 10 nor any other provision in this Agreement can be waived or amended except by written consent of both Parties. 2 <PAGE>
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that neither party has any legal obligation, while the changed text states that each party *does* have legal obligations. This directly contradicts the language in the 'Contradicted Text' section, as both sections now appear in the same location, creating a clear in-text contradiction regarding legal obligations.
